channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI makes edge computing more relevant to CIOs
item.description: Analysts predict the incoming phase of enterprise AI will herald agentic systems that require minimal human intervention, with 75% of CIOs increasing their AI budgets during this year alone, according to a recent report from Gartner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Making technology a company capability at Foot Locker
item.description: Retail is dynamic, ever-changing, and generates a lot of data, and through merchandising, in-store transactions, supply chain, digital, and pricing, the opportunities to leverage data are endless.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Spotify is adding new markets to its music video test
item.description: Spotify is expanding its beta test of adding music videos to the platform by several dozen regions, though it still is unavailable in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The Apple Watch 10 proves itself as a medical device
item.description: Reportedly in the works for future versions of the Apple Watch: Blood pressure monitoring and blood glucose readings.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, NewsNation

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cyber criminals are increasingly helping Russia and China target the US and allies, Microsoft says
item.description: Russia, China and Iran are expanding their partnerships with cyber criminals to conduct cyberespionage and cyberattacks against the U.S. and other nations, according to a new report from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Ernst & Young’s AI platform is ‘radically’ reshaping operations
item.description: Multinational consultancy Ernst & Young (EY) said generative AI (genAI) is “radically reshaping” the way it operates, and the company boasts a 96% adoption rate of the technology by employees. After spending $1.4 billion on a customized generative AI platform called EY.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What in the world is going on at Ubisoft?
item.description: Ubisoft is no stranger to takeover whispers. In 2004, Electronic Arts made a run at the video game publisher, but was ultimately rebuffed. In 2015, Vivendi made an even more serious attempt that took years to defuse.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: How do we fund open source?
item.description: The world has come to rely upon the free work of millions of skilled software developers—the maintainers of free open-source software (FOSS) projects. But the world hasn’t given them a tip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs
item.description: Michael Berkholtz, a senior manager for technology lifecycle services for GSA, said the agency is analyzing emerging technologies based on four criteria. The post GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
